_meta.note: Illustrative subset shipped so the MCP server is functional out of the box. Control text is abridged. CMMC L2 mappings are illustrative crosswalks to CMMC 2.0 Level 2 / NIST SP 800-171 practice IDs, not an authoritative mapping.

AC-2.title: Account Management
AC-2.text: Manage system accounts: define account types; assign account managers; establish conditions for membership; authorize, create, enable, modify, disable, and remove accounts per policy; and monitor account use.

AC-3.title: Access Enforcement
AC-3.text: Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access control policies.

AU-2.title: Event Logging
AU-2.text: Identify the event types the system is capable of logging in support of the audit function; coordinate the logging function with other organizational entities; and review/update logged event types periodically.

AU-6.title: Audit Record Review, Analysis, and Reporting
AU-6.text: Review and analyze system audit records for indications of inappropriate or unusual activity, and report findings to designated personnel. Adjust review frequency based on risk.

CM-7.title: Least Functionality
CM-7.text: Configure the system to provide only mission-essential capabilities; prohibit or restrict the use of unnecessary functions, ports, protocols, software, and services.

IA-2.title: Identification and Authentication (Organizational Users)
IA-2.text: Uniquely identify and authenticate organizational users and associate that identity with processes acting on their behalf; implement multifactor authentication for network access to privileged and non-privileged accounts.

IA-5.title: Authenticator Management
IA-5.text: Manage system authenticators by verifying identity before issuance; establishing initial content; enforcing strength and lifetime; protecting authenticators from disclosure; and changing default authenticators.

RA-5.title: Vulnerability Monitoring and Scanning
RA-5.text: Monitor and scan for vulnerabilities in the system and hosted applications; analyze reports; remediate legitimate vulnerabilities within defined timeframes; and share information with designated personnel.

SC-7.title: Boundary Protection
SC-7.text: Monitor and control communications at the external managed interfaces and key internal interfaces of the system; implement subnetworks for publicly accessible components; and connect to external networks only through managed interfaces (boundary protection devices).

SI-2.title: Flaw Remediation
SI-2.text: Identify, report, and correct system flaws; test software and firmware updates for effectiveness and side effects before installation; and install security-relevant updates within defined timeframes.
